summary: Kenneth Suna demonstrates how to sell cash-secured puts as part of a premium-selling strategy, using HIMS earnings as a high-risk example. He sold 3 contracts at $57 strike for $814 premium (4.7% return) while managing downside risk by reserving $17,100 in capital. The video includes a step-by-step tutorial on executing cash-secured put trades via broker platform.
takeaways: Sell cash-secured puts on stocks you're comfortable owning long-term
Strike selection: choose strikes where stock would need to drop dramatically (e.g., HIMS at $64, sold $57 puts)
Premium must compensate for max loss: $35 premium + $55.95 net cost = $59 strike price
Reserve full capital requirement upfront: selling 3 contracts at $57 requires $17,100 reserved in account
Avoid greedy exits: lock in $193 on Oscar Health rather than chase extra $10-15 per contract
Earnings trades are high-risk, suitable only for experienced traders (15+ years trading)
Weekly target: $1,200 premium on $50k portfolio (~5% return for the week)

strategy_candidates.change: Integrate cash-secured puts into rotation with covered calls when underlying drops below target entry, reserving capital systematically to reach $1,250/mo target
strategy_candidates.evidence: Kenneth made $1,200 this week using CSPs + covered calls on limited capital deployed ($3,100 actual outlay due to CSP capital reservation). Demonstrates CSPs defer capital outlay while capturing premium, freeing up funds for additional positions to meet monthly premium goals.

title: Part 10: 8/4/25 $180 a week and HOW TO SELL CASH SECURED PUTS
